7th Foundry

Seven, Forged Into One
Scroll
01 / Manifesto
Seven specialists.
One standard. One craft.

Great brands are never assembled.
They're forged.

7th Foundry began with seven friends, each completing a Master's in Digital and Strategic Marketing Management, each carrying a different discipline into the room. Rather than start seven separate practices, they chose one shared foundry, a place where raw, individual skill is tested under shared pressure and comes out stronger, sharper, and more complete than it started.

The name carries two ideas at once: the number seven, which needs nothing added to feel whole, and the foundry, where heat, pressure, and time turn separate material into a single, permanent form.

How raw material becomes identity

03 / The Mark

At first glance, it's architecture. Look again, and it's alphabet.

The symbol begins with a 7, not hidden, but built into the very bones of the mark. Seven has always meant completion. Seven days to a week. Seven notes to a scale. Seven colors in light itself. It's the number that doesn't need an eighth to feel whole. When seven individuals came together to found this agency, they weren't looking for a group, they were looking for a complete set. The 7 in this mark is that idea made visible: a shape that already feels finished before it's even in motion.

Sitting inside that same structure is the F, for Foundry. It doesn't sit beside the 7, competing for space. It's fused into it, sharing strokes, sharing weight. That's deliberate. This was never seven people and a separate company name bolted on afterward. The name and the number were forged together, at the same time, under the same pressure, which is exactly how the agency itself came to be.

"Structure gives way to flow, and flow settles back into structure."

And then, the turn. Where the mark could have stayed rigid, all hard edges and right angles, it doesn't. It bends into a curve that reads almost like an S before it resolves, a flowing line that moves through the solid form rather than around it. This is the moment of transformation: the heat, the pressure, the pour. It's the visual record of becoming, raw material in one state, entering the form as one thing, and leaving as another. Seven separate people, put through the same fire, coming out the other side as one.

There is a fourth element, easy to miss at first: the small circle of fire sitting just outside the frame, top left. It is not decoration. It is the moment before the forge starts, the spark that pulls everything else into motion. Seven individuals bring seven kinds of energy, seven ways of working, seven different fires. The circle is all of that compressed into a single point: force, heat, and intent, arriving before the shape even begins to form. Everything the mark becomes starts here.

7
Completion
F
Fused In
S
Transformation
●
The Spark

05 / Variations
For Team Review

Lockup options

Six wordmark and colour directions, for the team to shortlist. Each pairs the mark with a different treatment of "7TH FOUNDRY."

Option 1
One line lockup, forest green
Option 2
One line lockup, gold script accent on "7th"
Option 3
Stacked lockup, forest green
Option 4
Stacked, gold script accent on "7th"
Option 5
Stacked, gold script, tighter kerning
Option 6
Full gold gradient wordmark

08 / Typography
Networkand & Mont
Aa
7TH IN NETWORKAND · FOUNDRY IN MONT
Display: "7th" (Networkand)
7th

Reserved for the numeral treatment and hero moments only, never for body copy or UI. The actual Networkand typeface, embedded directly.

Wordmark: "Foundry" (Mont)
FOUNDRY

A clean geometric sans for the wordmark, headings, and all UI labels. Poppins is used here as the closest open license match to Mont.

09 / Grid
Structure

8pt grid, 12 column system

All spacing in multiples of 8px. Margins scale from 24px (mobile) to 96px (desktop).
10 / Photography
Mood & Direction

Real process, real craft

Creative teams at work, hands sketching, architecture, steel, fire, material, imagery in the spirit of Aesop, Apple, Notion, and Monocle. Authentic and unposed, never stock photography energy.

Hands & Craft
Warm Fire Tones

12 / Voice
How We Sound

We say / We don't say

We SayWe Don't Say
We solve.We try.
Built with intent.Made quickly.
Designed to endure.Designed to trend.
Strategy before aesthetics.Aesthetics without purpose.
Competent

We know our craft and it shows. Not claimed, demonstrated.

Trustworthy

We take clients where they actually need to go, not just where's easiest for us.

Honest

We tell clients the truth, even when it's not what they want to hear. That's what keeps trust long after the first project ends.
